title: Living Together
cefrDescriptors: Can express obligation and necessity with il faut.
Can state rules and prohibitions and give simple instructions.
concept:
Living with other people means rules — and French has a neat, impersonal way to state them: il faut.

il faut + infinitive = "you have to / one must / it's necessary to." It never changes form (always il faut), and it's followed by a verb in the infinitive:
  Il faut payer le loyer. (You have to pay the rent.)
  Il faut respecter les voisins. (You must respect the neighbours.)

To forbid something, make it negative — il ne faut pas + infinitive = "you must not":
  Il ne faut pas faire de bruit. (You mustn't make noise.)

For official-sounding rules and signs, French uses il est interdit de + infinitive (it is forbidden to…):
  Il est interdit de fumer. (No smoking.)

And you already met l'impératif in the directions unit — it's still the quickest way to give a direct instruction (tu / vous / nous), with no subject pronoun:
  Range ta chambre ! (Tidy your room!)   Faisons le ménage. (Let's do the housework.)

The shared-living vocabulary fits the theme: le loyer (rent), le voisin (neighbour), le bruit (noise), la règle (rule), la poubelle (the bin), and verbs like partager (to share), ranger (to tidy), respecter (to respect), faire le ménage (to do the housework).

A set of house rules in action:
  « Il faut ranger la cuisine. Il ne faut pas faire de bruit le soir. Il est interdit de fumer. »

Write two rules for a shared flat: one with il faut and one with il ne faut pas.

That's the final module of A1 — félicitations !

vocabulary.exampleEn: You have to pay the rent.

vocabulary.exampleFr: Il y a trop de bruit.
Sors la poubelle, s'il te plaît.
Il est interdit de fumer ici.
Je fais le ménage le samedi.
vocabulary.exampleEn: I do the housework on Saturdays.

examples.en: You have to pay the rent.

examples.fr: Il ne faut pas faire de bruit après 22 heures.
examples.en: You mustn't make noise after 10 pm.

examples.fr: Range ta chambre, s'il te plaît.

examples.en: You have to respect the neighbours.
